Thanks for contributing to Course: Computer Science!

## Summary

<!-- What does this PR do, and why? 1–3 sentences. -->

Closes #<!-- issue number, if applicable -->

## Type of change

<!-- Put an x in all that apply. -->

- [ ] 📚 Lesson content (text in `docs/*.md`)
- [ ] 🧩 Lesson code or artifact (`code/`, `outputs/`)
- [ ] 🌍 Translation (UI locale JSON or `docs/<locale>.md`)
- [ ] 🐛 Website / build bug fix
- [ ] ✨ Feature / enhancement
- [ ] 📖 Documentation / tooling
- [ ] 🧹 Refactor / chore (no behavior change)

## How it was tested

<!-- Show the commands you ran and their result. "It builds" is not a test. -->

- [ ] `node --test` passes
- [ ] `node site/build.js` succeeds (if site/content changed)
- [ ] Verified in a browser (for UI changes) — note locale/RTL if relevant
- [ ] Lesson code runs as described (for code changes)

```
# paste relevant command output here
```

## Screenshots / recordings

<!-- For any visible UI change, include before/after. Delete if not applicable. -->

## Contributor checklist

- [ ] PR is focused on a single logical change
- [ ] No `TODO`, `NotImplementedError`, `unimplemented!`, or `scaffold-stub` markers left behind
- [ ] Translations keep all keys identical to `en.json`, preserve placeholders, and use no raw `"` inside values
- [ ] If curriculum structure changed, `README.md` / `ROADMAP.md` / phase `README.md` were updated together
- [ ] I read the [Contributing Guide](../blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md) and followed the Code of Conduct
